Paul Heckingbottom makes five changes to the site that beat Portsmouth 2-1 two weeks ago. Out go Woodman, Kesler-Harden, Porteos, Lindsay and Ledson; in come Cornell, Hughes, Storey, Brady and Whiteman. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Preston North End vs. Aston Villa team news: Unai Emery makes Ollie Watkins decision for FA Cup quarter-final tie
arts.sum: Preston North End and Aston Villa announce their starting lineups for Sunday's FA Cup quarter-final at Deepdale.

arts.ttl: Martínez set for 200th Villa appearance at Preston
arts.sum: Fixtures Preston North End Vs. Aston Villa - Sun, Mar 30th 2025, 13:30 Emi Martínez will make his 200th appearance for Aston Villa this afternoon, as Unai Emery’s side take on Preston North End in the FA Cup quarter-finals. The Argentinian goalkeeper captains the team behind a back four of Lucas Digne, Tyrone Mings, Ezri Konsa and Matty Cash at Deepdale, as the Villans bid to book their place in a Wembley semi-final. Emery has opted for Boubacar Kamara and Youri Tielemans in midfield, for Villa's first game out of the international break. Morgan Rogers, Marco Asensio and Jacob Ramsey line up alongside Marcus Rashford in attack, with a total of three changes from Villa’s last outing before the break - their 3-0 victory over Club Brugge at Villa Park in the Champions League round of 16 second leg. Aston Villa: Martínez (c), Cash, Konsa, Mings, Digne, Tielemans, Kamara, Rogers, Asensio, Ramsey, Rashford. Subs: Olsen, Pau, García, Maatsen, Bogarde, Onana, McGinn, Malen, Watkins. Preston: Cornell, Meghoma, Whiteman (c), Keane, Riis, Frøkjær, Brady, Storey, Hughes, Gibson, Thórdarson. Subs: Stowell, Lindsay, Bauer, Pasiek, Tarry, Carroll, Mawane, Evans, Osmajić.

arts.ttl: Preston vs Aston Villa LIVE: Team news and line-ups before FA Cup quarter-final today
arts.sum: Villa face the only Championship side left in the competition, with a place in a Wembley semi-final at stake

arts.ttl: Goalkeeper 'who will have incredible career' makes breakthrough after £1m Aston Villa transfer
arts.sum: Aston Villa sold goalkeeper Viljami Sinisalo to Scottish champions Celtic in the summer transfer window, after helping to develop the young Fin and having provided him with a series of fruitful loan spells. With Villa having signed Joe Gauci midway through last season, though, and the highly-rated Oliwier Zych making strides in the first-team environment, it was decided that Sinisalo would leave permanently in the summer.

arts.sum: Aston Villa and Newcastle United face slim chances of signing Eintracht Frankfurt striker Hugo Ekitike this summer, with Arsenal, Liverpool, and Manchester United all showing strong interest in securing his services. According to Sky Germany, his club is bracing for Ekitike’s departure in the upcoming transfer window. arts.ttl: 'Being awkward' - Preston manager confirms Aston Villa truth after FA Cup blow - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Preston North End manager Paul Heckingbottom has confirmed that Kaine Kesler-Hayden is unable to play against his parent club this afternoon due to FA rules, rather than Aston Villa "being awkward". However, Kesler-Hayden will be unavailable for today's FA Cup semi-final against Villa—a situation Heckingbottom admits is frustrating, as he is unable to select one of his first-choice defenders.
